Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Department of Justice Act

Provision.Text: The bill amends the Department of Justice Act to, among other things, require that the Minister of Justice cause to be tabled, for every government bill introduced in either House of Parliament, a statement of the bill√¢¬Ä¬ôs potential effects on the rights of Indigenous Peoples that are recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.
Whereas section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982 states, √¢¬Ä¬úThe existing aboriginal and treaty rights of the aboriginal peoples of Canada are hereby recognized and affirmed.√¢¬Ä¬ù;

Whereas subsection 8.3(1) of the Interpretation Act states, √¢¬Ä¬úEvery enactment is to be construed as upholding the Aboriginal and treaty rights of Indigenous peoples recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982, and not as abrogating or derogating from them.√¢¬Ä¬ù;
Whereas the Government of Canada is committed to implementing the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples, including the principle of free, prior and informed consent;

Whereas Article 19 of the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples declares, √¢¬Ä¬úStates shall consult and cooperate in good faith with the indigenous peoples concerned through their own representative institutions in order to obtain their free, prior and informed consent before adopting and implementing legislative or administrative measures that may affect them.√¢¬Ä¬ù;

Whereas section 5 of the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act states, √¢¬Ä¬úThe Government of Canada must, in consultation and cooperation with Indigenous peoples, take all measures necessary to ensure that the laws of Canada are consistent with the Declaration.√¢¬Ä¬ù;

Whereas the duty to consult requires the Government of Canada to consult Indigenous Peoples in good faith before taking actions that may adversely affect their asserted or established rights under section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982;

Whereas Parliament recognizes that First Nations, Inuit and M√É¬©tis Peoples have their own distinctive collective identities, cultures, languages and ways of life and that recognition of these distinctions is essential to any meaningful consultation;

Whereas an Indigenous Rights Statement is a transparency mechanism for parliamentarians to understand whether a government bill may infringe Indigenous Peoples√¢¬Ä¬ô rights and whether adequate consultation and engagement have occurred;
And whereas the presentation of an Indigenous Rights Statement to Parliament does not relieve the government of Canada from its duty to consult;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Indigenous Rights Statement Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 4.2(1) of the Department of Justice Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister shall, for every Bill introduced in or presented to either House of Parliament by a minister or other representative of the Crown, cause to be tabled, in the House in which the Bill originates, a statement that sets out

Paragraph.Text: potential effects of the Bill on

Subparagraph.Text: the rights and freedoms that are guaranteed by the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the rights of Indigenous peoples that are recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whether the Bill is consistent with the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples.

Subsection.Text: If the statement sets out potential effects on the rights referred to in subparagraph (1)(a)(ii), the statement must also include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a detailed summary of any consultations undertaken with the Indigenous peoples who hold these rights; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an indication that no consultations were undertaken with the Indigenous peoples referred to in paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: Section 4.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
For the purposes of subsections (1) and (1.1), Indigenous peoples has the meaning assigned by the definition aboriginal peoples of Canada in subsection 35(2) of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Section.Text: This Act comes into force on the first anniversary of the day on which it receives royal assent.
